# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Suggested Final Summary

Replied:
- email_id:
  - why it needed a reply:

Skipped:
- email_id:
  - why it did not need a reply:

# Inbox Workflow Context

This task simulates a lightweight customer-support inbox workflow.

Available context:
- `policy.md` explains when to reply and how to phrase responses
- `faq.md` contains the approved answers you can use
- `emails/inbox.json` contains the messages to triage

Expected behavior:
- reply to actionable questions
- skip messages that do not need follow-up
- save all reply drafts through the fake reply tool

# Fake Email Workflow Fixture

This task simulates a small inbox triage and reply workflow.

Useful files:
- `context.md` explains the task context
- `policy.md` defines when a reply is needed
- `faq.md` contains the approved answers you can reuse
- `emails/inbox.json` contains the fake inbox
- `output_template.md` suggests how to summarize your actions

Important:
- replies must be saved with the fake reply tool
- do not reply to non-actionable confirmation or thank-you messages

[{"id": "email_001", "from": "alex@example.com", "subject": "Need confirmation on the workshop date"}, {"id": "email_002", "from": "billing@example.com", "subject": "Invoice received"}, {"id": "email_003", "from": "maria@example.com", "subject": "Need the onboarding checklist"}, {"id": "email_004", "from": "sam@example.com", "subject": "Thanks for the quick help"}]

{"id": "email_001", "from": "alex@example.com", "subject": "Need confirmation on the workshop date", "body": "Can you confirm whether the workshop is still planned for next Thursday? If the schedule changed, please let me know the new time."}

payload.output_preview:
Draft replies created for the actionable emails below.

Reply draft for email_001 (to: alex@example.com)
Subject: Re: Need confirmation on the workshop date
Body:
Yes — the workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET. If the schedule changes, I will notify you right away.

Reply draft for email_003 (to: maria@example.com)
Subject: Re: Need the onboarding checklist
Body:
The onboarding checklist is in the docs portal under Getting Started > Pilot Setup: https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup. If you’d like, I can also send the checklist directly or schedule a quick walkthrough.

Skipped (no reply needed)
- email_002 (billing@example.com): This is only a payment confirmation — no action requested.
- email_004 (sam@example.com): Thank-you note stating no action needed.

Notes:
- Replies follow the local policy and FAQ (workshop time and docs portal URL used).
- Replies are concise, polite, and specific.

payload.output_preview:
Review summary:

Replied (drafts saved and acceptable)
- email_001 (alex@example.com): Draft confirms the workshop is planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET and promises notification if it changes. Tone and length meet policy; direct answer first and a concrete next step are present. No changes needed.
- email_003 (maria@example.com): Draft gives the docs portal path and URL and offers to send the checklist or schedule a walkthrough. Concise, direct, and matches the FAQ. No changes needed.

Skipped (no reply needed)
- email_002 (billing@example.com): Payment confirmation only — correctly skipped per policy.
- email_004 (sam@example.com): Thank-you note with no action requested — correctly skipped per policy.

No further replies required.

final_output_text:
Review summary:

Replied (drafts saved and acceptable)
- email_001 (alex@example.com): Draft confirms the workshop is planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET and promises notification if it changes. Tone and length meet policy; direct answer first and a concrete next step are present. No changes needed.
- email_003 (maria@example.com): Draft gives the docs portal path and URL and offers to send the checklist or schedule a walkthrough. Concise, direct, and matches the FAQ. No changes needed.

Skipped (no reply needed)
- email_002 (billing@example.com): Payment confirmation only — correctly skipped per policy.
- email_004 (sam@example.com): Thank-you note with no action requested — correctly skipped per policy.

No further replies required.

manual_eval.score_notes: Best multi-agent run here: correct replies, good tone, and a concise review summary, though still costlier than single-agent execution.
